title: lemna tenera
intro: Lemna tenera, also known as the lesser duckweed, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the Lemnaceae family, characterized by its small, round leaves and ability to grow in shallow water. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Lemna tenera include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Lemna tenera is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or taken sublingually.
